name: Arrow Bank National Association
description_short: Arrow Bank National Association is a full-service community bank offering checking, savings, mortgages, business lending, and wealth management services across upstate New York.
description_long:
Arrow Bank National Association is a community bank based in Glens Falls, New York, with a 175-year history of service to upstate New York and surrounding regions. The bank is part of Arrow Financial Corporation and operates as a traditional banking institution with a focus on relationship-based banking for both personal and commercial customers.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, CDs, and On Call Banking products. On the borrowing side, Arrow Bank provides mortgages, home equity loans, personal loans, and credit cards. Their business division serves companies with business checking, business savings, payment solutions, municipal banking, cash management services, business lending, and credit cards. The wealth management division includes asset management, investment services through LPL Financial, individual and employer retirement plans, trusts and estates planning, and insurance services.

Arrow Bank distinguishes itself through its long operational history and consistent financial ratings. The bank has received a 5-Star Superior rating from BauerFinancial for 75 consecutive quarters, positioning itself as an "Exceptional Performance Bank." The institution also demonstrates community commitment through significant philanthropic efforts, exceeding $1 million in annual giving in 2025 and volunteering over 13,700 hours. The bank is recognized as a top SBA lender in the Capital Region and offers specialized products like the Hero Mortgage Program for essential workers.

As a traditional community bank, Arrow Bank is best suited for customers seeking personal service and local relationships rather than the lowest rates. The bank's strengths lie in its stability, community integration, and comprehensive service offerings. However, as a regional institution, it may have limited geographic accessibility compared to national banks and may not compete on rates or digital-first features with newer fintech competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: 75 consecutive quarters of 5-Star Superior ratings from BauerFinancial, demonstrating exceptional financial stability
Comprehensive service suite including personal banking, mortgages, business lending, and wealth management in one institution
Hero Mortgage Program specifically designed for essential workers and community heroes
Recognized as a top SBA lender in the Capital Region with demonstrated commitment to small business support
Strong community engagement with $1+ million in annual charitable giving and 13,700+ volunteer hours
Full digital capabilities including mobile app, online banking, Zelle money transfer, and digital wallet integration
175-year operational history providing established trust and institutional knowledge
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a regional bank, not accessible to customers outside upstate New York markets
No mention of competitive rate leadership or rate guarantees compared to national and online banks
Limited transparency on fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or APY rates on savings products visible on website
No indication of specialized high-yield savings or premium checking products that compete with newer fintech offerings
Website does not highlight customer service response times or satisfaction metrics
best_for: Upstate New York residents seeking a stable, locally-oriented bank with comprehensive personal and business services
Small business owners interested in relationship-based lending and SBA loan programs
Customers prioritizing bank stability and community involvement over rate competitiveness
Essential workers (healthcare, emergency services, teachers) interested in the Hero Mortgage Program
diagnosis: Arrow Bank is best for upstate New York residents and businesses seeking a stable, full-service community bank with strong financial ratings and local relationship banking. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only serves upstate New York markets—and it may not offer the most competitive rates or cutting-edge digital features compared to national and online-only banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
